item.title: UK's choice for wind operators: a new deal or higher taxes
item.description: UK energy secretary Ed Milliband says operators of older plants face 'significantly changed economic incventives' under revamped approach

item.title: Under threat: European wind foundation makers accuse China's Dajin of dodging dumping rules
item.description: German offshore wind foundation manufacturers accuses industrial giant of building purpose-built fleet to transport monopiles built with dumped Chinese steel

item.title: Traditional offshore wind grid links are on the way out: TenneT chief
item.description: Offshore wind must be linked to countries in ‘smarter’ way than it has been to date, says TenneT Germany chief

item.title: Accelerate buildout and back PPAs if you want cheaper renewable electricity, wind chief says
item.description: As the UK prepares to unveil gas decoupling measures WindEurope chief shows she is is wary of interventionism

item.title: Lies about wind power are 'systemic risk to security' - WindEurope
item.description: New study shows how false narratives circulating in social media and real life lead of project delays and cancellations, costing billions of euros

item.title: New UK offshore wind record claimed by Iberdrola and Masdar
item.description: Siemens Gamesa wind turbine blade was installed by Cadeler jack-up vessel at East Anglia Three project in milestone moment for UK renewables

item.title: UK to push older wind onto CfDs in bid to break gas price link
item.description: Measures will reportedly include offer of new CfD for projects on Renewables Obligation - and higher windfall levy for those that remain

item.title: WindEurope's 10-point plan to boost wind power in face of Iran energy crisis
item.description: Fast-track permitting, repowering ageing wind farms and ramping up funding for grids all crucial to helping boost Europe’s deployment of renewables, says WindEurope

item.title: Enercon profitable again but CEO rules out discounts amid falling onshore tender prices
item.description: Udo Bauer says order intake of 7MW turbine model is 'above plan' and manufacturer sees hybrid offerings with energy storage giving clients better profits than ever higher power ratings

item.title: Another setback for Taiwan offshore wind as EDF scraps project
item.description: French developer is fifth winner in opening Round 3.1 to terminate development amid skyrocketing costs and strict local content requirement

item.title: Battery storage now fastest-growing power technology: IEA
item.description: Electricity use rose despite sluggish energy demand against a complex geopolitical backdrop, the report says

item.title: Madrid summit: Wind’s record expansion at risk as political intervention threatens revenue
item.description: Political expertise might be more important than ever as the European wind industry meets in Madrid

item.title: US wind turbine maker racks up Spanish order despite tensions
item.description: New York-based GE Vernova in deal to sell its 6.1MW onshore units as tensions remain between Spain and US over Iran conflict

item.title: Total boss rules out three more offshore wind markets after US exit
item.description: Patrick Pouyanné's latest blow to the offshore wind sector risks leading the sector to concentrate risk in only a handful of countries, says industry expert

item.title: Wind stakes claim as energy crisis-buster with record growth
item.description: GWEC says energy source saw 40% capacity expansion last year driven by onshore and China

item.title: 'Wind farm' built on Donald Trump's favourite golf course
item.description: Greenpeace campaigners storm one of US president's Scottish courses to protest against his energy policy

item.title: Are cracks emerging between European wind groups over Chinese turbines?
item.description: With a German offshore wind group’s decision to accept Mingyang as a member having turned heads throughout the sector, Recharge asked what other European wind associations think about Chinese turbines

item.title: UK power shares fall after government flags price shake-up
item.description: SSE, Centrica and Orsted among those hit after Reeves says country will look to break link between power and gas prices

item.title: Judge orders GE Vernova to stay on Vineyard offshore wind project
item.description: Order issued Friday enjoins US wind turbine manufacturer from stopping work on ‘forever first’ array developed by Spain's Iberdrola and Copenhagen Infrastructure Partners as dispute goes through court

item.title: Judge appears open to blocking GE Vernova from abandoning Iberdrola’s Vineyard Wind
item.description: Massachusetts judge casts doubt on GE Vernova's right to abandon Vineyard Wind but delays ruling on temporary restraining order

item.title: Deals of the Week: Equinor, Amazon, RWE and Envision
item.description: Norway's Equinor sold off shares in renewable energy developer Scatec, leading to speculation it could be precursor to jettisoning stake in offshore wind giant Orsted

item.title: EDP parks US offshore wind projects to 'wait for better days' as rivals retreat
item.description: Developer with Engie in 2.4GW SouthCoast array signals notable optimism as TotalEnergies gives up on sector, with RWE expected to follow

item.title: Four key arguments GE Vernova has raised in Vineyard Wind dispute
item.description: US wind turbine manufacturer says offshore wind Iberdrola-Copenhagen Infrastructure Partners project company is trying to 'rewrite contract' and that alleged harm should it quit work would be 'self-inflicted'

item.title: UK Supreme Court's Orsted ruling a setback for wind and solar, say experts
item.description: Ruling comes at a time when cost pressures and financing constraints are already making life difficult in building new wind farms and solar arrays, say legal experts

item.title: Why China wants its clean tech champions to calm down
item.description: Battery leader CATL ramps up its capacity guidance despite government efforts to dampen competition in the sector

item.title: First US state passes data centre ban over power fears – but the reality is complex
item.description: Steep power price hikes are often blamed on AI, but Maine has not seen large loads come online and its 55% rate hike is more linked to natural gas shortfalls

item.title: Chinese foundations 'drive down European wind costs', but one supplier wants to go further
item.description: Chinese foundations have already carved out a share of European offshore wind market and now Dajin Heavy Industry wants to offer a one-stop shop services including transport and installation

item.title: Is Orsted next on the block as Equinor sells renewables stake?
item.description: Analysts ponder implications for oil giant's holding in Danish offshore wind group as it divests half of its shares in Scatec

item.title: GE Vernova hits back in Vineyard court battle
item.description: US turbine manufacturer claimed 'forever first' project owes it money and is using litigation to gain commercial advantage

item.title: Offshore wind to power Britain's railways
item.description: Network Rail to take power from RWE wind farm under new power deal that marks further uptick for European sector
